title: calceolaria ternata
intro: Calceolaria ternata, also known as the 'slipper flower', is a perennial plant native to the Andes mountains in South America. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Calceolaria ternata include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory issues, and exhibit antioxidant properties. Herbal preparations of Calceolaria ternata can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, but more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety for human consumption.
